reasoning: The FRED auto loans series shows a strong upward momentum over the last few months (rising consistently from 485.5 in March 2025 to 491.1 in June 2025). As a seasonally adjusted macroeconomic time series, the most recent trend acts as a strong short-term indicator, meaning the value is likelier than not to remain above or continue rising from its June 22 baseline in the near future. While the linear trend tool suggests mean-reversion, economic series behave closer to random walks with drift, making continuation from the most recent level more probable. Following the explicit instructions to ensure uncertainty increases (getting closer to 0.5) with longer horizons, my forecasts start at 0.65 for the near-term and gradually revert toward 0.51 for December 2025.
